title: Ancient Scrolls Reveal Their Secrets
story: A clever computer student cracked a 2,000-year-old mystery on October 12, 2023. Using a smart computer program, they read words from Roman scrolls burnt by Mount Vesuvius long ago. These ancient papers from Herculaneum had been unreadable until now. Amazingly, the first word decoded was 'purple'! This breakthrough lets us peek into the past and learn what ancient Romans wrote about.
activity_question: What was the first word deciphered from the ancient Roman scrolls?

title: Mars Reveals Its Icy Secret
story: Cameras clicked as Mariner 9 zoomed above the Red Planet. In 1972, this robotic explorer captured the first clear images of Mars' north pole. They showed that even this alien desert had frozen water, hinting at the possibility of Martian life.

title: Magellan's Final Message from Venus
story: Silence fell across space as NASA lost contact with the Magellan spacecraft on October 12, 1994. After four years of mapping Venus, this robotic explorer plunged into the planet's thick atmosphere. Magellan's mission ended, but not before it unveiled Venus' secrets, mapping nearly all of the planet's surface and revealing volcanoes, impact craters, and strange lava formations.
activity_question: What did the Magellan spacecraft do before losing contact?
activity_choices: Mapped nearly all of Venus' surface
activity_answer: Mapped nearly all of Venus' surface
bonus_fact: Venus is often called Earth's 'evil twin' because of its similar size but extreme heat and pressure.

title: Challenger's Big Move for Space Mission
story: The space shuttle Challenger rolled into its giant garage in 1985. On October 12, it entered the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. This massive structure, taller than a skyscraper, was where NASA prepared shuttles for their missions. Challenger was getting ready for its next adventure in space, called STS-61A.
activity_question: Where did the Challenger move for maintenance?
bonus_fact: The Vehicle Assembly Building is so big, it has its own weather inside!

title: A President's Peace Prize
story: Cheers erupted in Costa Rica! Their president, Oscar Arias, had just won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1987. On October 12, the world celebrated this leader's efforts to bring peace to Central America. Arias worked tirelessly to end conflicts in neighboring countries, showing that talking things out is better than fighting.
activity_question: What did Oscar Arias win?
follow_up_question: Why do you think peace is important for countries?

title: Al Gore's Nobel Prize Surprise
story: Guess who became a Nobel Peace Prize winner? Al Gore, the former US Vice President! On October 12, 2007, Gore received this amazing honor. He shared the prize with a group of climate experts called the IPCC. They won for their hard work teaching people about climate change and how to protect our planet.
activity_question: Who did Al Gore share the Nobel Peace Prize with?
follow_up_question: What does IPCC stand for?
